title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR ADDITIONAL PROSECUTORS IN THE PROSECUTION DIVISION OF THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, ENLARGING AND REORGANIZING ITS PROSECUTION STAFF, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES.

chunks.text:
S. NO. 389 H. NO. 9802 / 65 OG No. 16, 3900 (April 21, 1969)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 5184, September 08, 1967 ]

AN ACT PROVIDING FOR ADDITIONAL PROSECUTORS IN THE PROSECUTION DIVISION OF THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, ENLARGING AND REORGANIZING ITS PROSECUTION STAFF, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES.

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

Provided,

That each judicial district as organized under the provisions of Republic Act Numbered Two hundred ninety-six, otherwise known as the Judiciary Act of 1948, as amended, shall have one District State Prosecutor who shall be permanently appointed thereto: Provided, further, That the District State Prosecutors shall be under the supervision of the Chief of State Prosecutor.

"The qualifications of the Chief State Prosecutor and the Assistant Chief State Prosecutors shall be the same as those of a Judge of the Court of First Instance and their salaries are hereby fixed at P19.000.00 each

per annum;

. The qualifications and rank of the Senior State Prosecutor, State Prosecutor or District State Prosecutor

shall be the same as a provincial fiscal.

"SEC. 3. The Chief State Prosecutor, the Assistant Chief State Prosecutors, the Senior State Prosecutors, the State Prosecutors, the District State Prosecutors, as well as other prosecuting attorneys assigned in the interest of the public service to the Prosecution Staff, shall have the same powers as provincial or city fiscals as provided by law:

Provided,

SEC. 2. Any provision of law to the contrary notwithstanding, the members of the Prosecution Staff and complementary personnel as hereinabove provided shall receive the salary rates provided for in this Act:

Provided,

That no prosecutor, attorney or employee of the merged prosecution services at the time of the approval of this Act shall, as a consequence of the operation of this Act, be removed or separated from the service except for cause as provided by law:

Provided, further,

That the Secretary of Justice shall determine and establish the respective positions, rank and salaries of the incumbent state prosecutors and personnel:

Provided, further,

That no incumbent prosecutor or personnel shall be demoted in rank and salary:

Provided, finally,

That the incumbent prosecutors, attorneys and personnel shall receive their salary adjustments without the need of any new appointment.

SEC. 3. In addition to such sums as may have been provided in the current Appropriations Act, there is hereby appropriated out of any funds in the National Treasury not otherwise appropriated, such sums as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act.

SEC. 4. This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved, September 8, 1967.
